title: Ensure that the API server pod specification file permissions are set to 600 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the API server pod specification file permissions are set to 600 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that the API server pod specification file has permissions of 600 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: The API server pod specification file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the API server. You should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be writable by only the administrators on the system.
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml Verify that the permissions are 600 or more restrictive.
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, chmod 600 /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
input_row.default_value: By default, the kube-apiserver.yaml file has permissions of 640 .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions using a command to check file permissions on the Control Plane node, which typically requires access to the underlying host system. However, since the control is about the API server pod specification file permissions, it can be inferred that this is a configuration management issue that can be checked through Kubernetes API or configuration management tools.
- The rationale emphasizes the importance of restricting access to the API server pod specification file, which is a standard Kubernetes service property.
- The audit mentions a specific command that can be executed to check the permissions, but since it is about the API server configuration, we can automate the check for the existence and configuration of the API server manifest.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_pod_spec_file_permissions_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use Kubernetes API calls or kubectl commands to check the permissions of the API server pod specification file.
- The command `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o jsonpath='{.items[?(@.metadata.name=="kube-apiserver")].spec.containers[*].volumeMounts[?(@.name=="kube-apiserver")].mountPath}'` can be used to identify the mount path of the API server pod specification.
- Validate that the permissions of the file at the identified path are set to 600 or more restrictive.
- Pass/Fail criteria: The expected value for the file permissions should be 600 or more restrictive. If the permissions are less than 600, it would be a fail.
- Example: After identifying the path, use `stat -c %a <path_to_kube_apiserver.yaml>` to check the permissions and ensure they meet the required standard.
